response.justification: The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. The job demands 8 years of C++ experience as a minimum qualifier, yet the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with C++ absent entirely from the skills section. The primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not systems-level C++. While there is noteworthy experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic and some AWS/Kubernetes exposure, neither the depth of cloud compute platform ownership nor formal cloud/DevOps technical leadership is present, making the candidate ineligible against both minimum and preferred qualifications.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems exposure: built low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic
Broad infrastructure breadth: hands-on with Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, CI/CD pipelines, and GitLab administration across multiple roles
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing culture: organized Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and multi-day Selenium courses — signals mentoring capacity
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: roughly 6 months total (~2014–2015), versus the 8-year minimum — this alone disqualifies the application
No cloud compute platform ownership: AWS CDK usage for pricing logic at Optilyz does not constitute production-level cloud infrastructure architecture or leadership

No formal technical lead role for cloud or DevOps teams: staff engineer titles appear, but the resume describes individual contributor and open-source founder work, not leading a platform engineering organization
